claim: Claude.md loaded into every prompt turn is the highest-impact configuration; 40k characters can encode team patterns, best practices, and architectural standards
actionable: Create comprehensive claude.md with team patterns, coding standards, architecture guidelines; regenerate when practices evolve

claim: Sub-agents share prompt cache; running 5-10 parallel agents costs marginally more than one due to cache reuse, enabling massive parallelism
actionable: Use parallel sub-agents for independent tasks; leverage Git worktrees to isolate sub-agent work and avoid conflicts

claim: Permissioning system evolution: auto mode predicts user intent with ML classifier, avoiding constant prompts while maintaining safety guardrails
actionable: Use 'auto' permission mode (default); avoid dangerously_skip_permissions which is now deprecated; configure allowed commands once

claim: Five compaction modes manage context: micro-compact (time), context collapse (summarize spans), session memory (extract key context), full compact, PTL truncation
actionable: Use /compact proactively before context loss occurs; opt into 1M token window if quality is critical; don't rely on auto-compaction alone

claim: Large tool results stored to disk with 8KB preview; paste massive files and model sees only fragment - keep input focused and relevant
actionable: For large files, use tools to extract relevant excerpts before passing to model; don't rely on model seeing full file when uploaded

claim: Streaming interruption is cheap; stopping agent mid-execution avoids sunk cost fallacy and allows immediate course correction
actionable: Stop agents immediately if they go wrong; cost of interruption is negligible; resuming from checkpoint is more efficient than letting bad code generate

claim: Sessions are persistent and resumable; continuing a session preserves context and momentum; --continue and --resume flags enable session reuse
actionable: Never start fresh sessions for related work; resume prior sessions with --continue to maintain context and improve quality

claim: Claude Code has hooks for automation: pre/post tool-use, session start/end events allow automation like auto-updating docs on commit
actionable: Configure hooks to automate boring tasks like documentation updates, testing triggers, and formatting checks post-action

claim: Open-sourcing Claude Code source code enables other developers to build better harnesses, discover weaknesses, and improve open-source alternatives
actionable: Learn from Claude Code design patterns; apply to open-source harnesses; study weakness to inform security hardening
